answer:
The Lorentz force is given by \( \mathbf{F} = q(\mathbf{E} + \mathbf{v} \times \mathbf{B}) \).  
For \( \mathbf{v}_3 = v_0 \mathbf{i}_z \), the force has no magnetic contribution because \( \mathbf{v}_3 \times \mathbf{B} \) is perpendicular to \( \mathbf{i}_z \), so \( \mathbf{E} = E_0(\mathbf{i}_x + \mathbf{i}_y) \).  
Using \( \mathbf{v}_1 \) and \( \mathbf{v}_2 \) with this \( \mathbf{E} \) gives \( \mathbf{B} = B_0 \mathbf{i}_z \).  

\boxed{A}
